records.description: University of Twente says Microsoft Copilot is available for employees through a UT account.
records.howToObtain: Use the Copilot website with a UT account.
records.costToUser: Included within the university license according to the public source.

evidence.sourceTitle: Available for all employees: Microsoft Copilot - University of Twente
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Microsoft Copilot is now available for employees at the University of Twente; users can work with it on the Copilot website using a UT account, and Copilot is included within the university license.

records.description: Deakin lists Deakin GEM as its secure generative AI platform for learning.
records.howToObtain: Access Deakin GEM from the approved tools page or GEM landing page.
records.costToUser: Free according to Deakin's public source.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved genAI tools for learning - Deakin University
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Deakin provides a set of endorsed generative AI and digital learning tools and lists Deakin GEM as a secure genAI platform hosted in Australia.

records.description: Deakin lists Microsoft Copilot for web as an approved generative AI tool for learning.
records.howToObtain: Launch via DeakinEdge and log in to a Microsoft account.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved genAI tools for learning - Deakin University
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Deakin's approved genAI tools table lists Microsoft Copilot for web and says to launch it via DeakinEdge and log in to a Microsoft account.

records.description: UMD lists TerpAI as a Division of IT generative AI service for the UMD community.
records.howToObtain: Access TerpAI through UMD's AI Resources service page.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD lists TerpAI under AI Services from the Division of IT and describes it as a generative AI tool for accessible and affordable AI at the University of Maryland.

records.description: UMD lists UMD Virtual Agent as a university-developed generative AI chatbot service.
records.howToObtain: Request or provision a virtual agent through UMD's listed service path.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD describes the UMD Virtual Agent as a generative AI chatbot developed at the university, with virtual study assistant and departmental assistant agents.

records.description: UMD lists ChatGPT Enterprise as an AI service for staff, faculty, and students.
records.howToObtain: Use an authorized UMD ChatGPT Enterprise account or UMD's ChatGPT licensing path.
records.costToUser: UMD notes license charges are moving to a tier-based allocation model.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD says ChatGPT Enterprise at UMD provides staff, faculty, and students with access to OpenAI frontier models, with workspace restrictions for authorized accounts.

records.description: UMD lists Perplexity Enterprise Pro as a one-year pilot for faculty and staff.
records.howToObtain: Use UMD's Perplexity AI service information or support path for the pilot.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD says Perplexity Enterprise Pro is available as a one-year pilot to UMD faculty and staff.

records.description: UMD says Claude passed its Software Risk Management audit and is approved for full usage, though departments must purchase Teams directly.
records.howToObtain: Departments purchase Claude Teams plans directly through Anthropic until an institutional arrangement is available.
records.costToUser: Department purchase required; UMD notes Claude Teams seat pricing constraints.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD says Claude has passed Software Risk Management security, privacy, and accessibility audit and has been approved for full usage across the university, but there is no campus-wide enterprise agreement.

records.description: UMD lists Google Gemini as an AI-powered productivity assistant integrated into Google Workspace.
records.howToObtain: Use UMD's Google AI services and Gemini information paths.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD lists Google Gemini as an AI-powered productivity assistant integrated into Google Workspace and links to Google AI Services guidance.

records.description: UMD lists NotebookLM as a focused research and knowledge workspace built on Gemini models.
records.howToObtain: Use NotebookLM through UMD's Google AI services guidance.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD says NotebookLM provides a focused research and knowledge workspace built on Gemini models for uploading source materials and querying them.

records.description: UMD lists Microsoft 365 Copilot as an AI-powered productivity assistant in the Microsoft 365 environment.
records.howToObtain: Use UMD-managed Microsoft 365 Copilot licensing tiers where available by role and unit.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD says Microsoft 365 Copilot is available in licensed tiers that distinguish web-based and work-grounded experiences, with availability varying by license, role, and unit.

records.description: UMD lists Adobe Firefly as a generative AI tool in the Adobe Creative Cloud ecosystem.
records.howToObtain: Use UMD's Adobe Firefly service information path.
records.costToUser: Includes 1000 monthly generation credits according to UMD.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMD lists Adobe Firefly and notes it comes with 1000 credits for asset generation, reset monthly.

records.description: UMN lists Gemini in its approved AI tools comparison for available tools.
records.howToObtain: Access through UMN's Gemini with enterprise-grade data protection service path.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN says only certain tools are approved for University of Minnesota data and lists Gemini in its available tools comparison.

records.description: UMN lists Microsoft Copilot with enterprise data protection in its approved AI tools comparison.
records.howToObtain: Access through UMN's Microsoft Copilot with Enterprise Data Protection service path.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN says only certain tools are approved for University of Minnesota data and lists Microsoft Copilot in its available tools comparison.

records.description: UMN lists NotebookLM in its approved AI tools comparison.
records.howToObtain: Access through UMN's NotebookLM service path.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN's available tools comparison lists NotebookLM alongside Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and Zoom AI Companion.

records.description: UMN lists Zoom AI Companion in its approved AI tools comparison.
records.howToObtain: Access through UMN's Zoom AI Companion guidance path.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN's available tools comparison lists Zoom AI Companion as an approved tool with enterprise-grade data protection.

records.description: UMN explicitly says Claude is not approved for institutional data.
records.howToObtain: No UMN-approved institutional-data use path is published for this record.
records.costToUser: Not applicable.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN says other generative AI tools available online, for example Claude and Perplexity, are not approved for any institutional data at UMN, even when data is anonymized.

records.description: UMN explicitly says Perplexity is not approved for institutional data.
records.howToObtain: No UMN-approved institutional-data use path is published for this record.
records.costToUser: Not applicable.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: UMN says other generative AI tools available online, for example Claude and Perplexity, are not approved for any institutional data at UMN, even when data is anonymized.

records.description: UCalgary publishes secure Microsoft Copilot Chat access for users with a UCalgary IT account.
records.howToObtain: Sign in with UCalgary credentials in Edge, the Copilot Chat website, or the managed-device desktop icon.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot Chat - University of Calgary Information Technologies
evidence.evidenceSnippet: UCalgary says a secure version of Microsoft Copilot Chat is available for anyone with a UCalgary IT account and must be accessed with UCalgary credentials.

records.description: UF guidance says UF GPT/Microsoft Copilot is the only AI tool approved by UF Integrated Risk Management at the time of the source.
records.howToObtain: Use UF GPT/Microsoft Copilot with a GatorLink login.
records.costToUser: Not specified by the public source.

evidence.sourceTitle: Guidelines for EDIS Authors When Using AI Tools - University of Florida
evidence.evidenceSnippet: UF states that UF GPT/Microsoft Copilot, when used with a GatorLink login, is the only AI tool approved by UF Integrated Risk Management.
